hook.text: Some math operations are easy to do but almost impossible to undo.

buildup.text: Multiplying two primes takes a second. Factoring the product can take centuries.

discovery.text: These are called one-way functions. All public-key cryptography depends on them.

twist.text: Nobody has proven that true one-way functions exist. We just believe they're hard.

climax.text: If someone proved they don't exist, every digital lock in the world would break.

punchline.text: Security rests on problems nobody can prove are hard.

quiz.question: What is a one-way function?
options: Easy to compute forward but hard to reverse
A function that only runs once
